commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation. Ophni and Phinees, (1 Kings iv. ; Calmet) the origin of this calamity, (Haydock) and the high priest himself broke his neck. (Worthington) Mourn, plorabantur, intimates rather that the people did not mourn for him. But the Hebrew has this meaning also, fleverunt, (Montanus) sunt fletæ. Jerome) (Haydock) The widow of Phinees died on hearing the sad news; and Eusebius seems to think that grief killed Ophni's widow likewise.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
